records.notes: Dear Loser, I am the Director of Graduate Admissions and Placement at the University of Texas at Austin's Department of Government. Although we are unable to admit you at this time to our Ph.D. program, I am happy to inform you that the admissions committee was impressed by your application. We have placed you on a waitlist for Fall 2023, in the hope that some spots will open up in our incoming class. Our admissions process is very competitive, and the decisions are tailored as much to the mix of interests represented in the class as they are to the quality of the applicants. We do believe you could be successful here if it turns out we are able to admit you. If you have general requirements or process-related questions about our program, please feel free to contact our Graduate Coordinator, Chaz Nailor ([email protected]). Although unfortunately we could not admit you outright, we would very much like you to keep our program in mind, as you make decisions about your graduate career. We hope to make our decisions about the waitlisted candidates in late March/early April as we hear from our current admits, and definitely before April 15.

Thank you for submitting your application to the Political Science PhD program at Stanford University. At this time, we have placed you on a waitlist for admission to our program. We had many more highly qualified and impressive applicants than we were able to admit this year, so our admissions committee has selected a very small group to comprise an unranked waitlist. While we are unable to extend an offer of admission to you at this time, you were among the best of our applicants and we hope that we will be able to offer you admission at a later date. The timing of our final admissions decision will depend upon when we hear from the students already admitted. Often we don’t hear decisions from admitted students until the April 15 response deadline, so we may not be able to give you a final decision about your waitlist status until that date. Once we have heard from the majority of the admitted students, we will begin contacting waitlisted candidates directly. In the meantime, if you decide to accept an offer from another institution or do not want to remain on our waitlist, please let us know so that we can remove you from the list.
